title: read_json treats milliseconds as microseconds when decimal part has only 3 digits
description:
### What happens?

When reading a JSON file that has a timestamp column with milliseconds precision (3 digits in fractional seconds), then the milliseconds are treated as microseconds (3 leading zeros are added).


### To Reproduce

```
create table s (test varchar, ts varchar);

insert into s values 
('no_trailing_zeroes', '2024-11-28 23:59:00.312'), 
('trailing_zeroes',       '2024-11-28 23:59:00.312000');

copy (from s) to s.json;
select test, ts, date_part('microseconds', ts) as microseconds from read_json('s.json');
```

Expected result:
```
┌────────────────────┬─────────────────────────┬──────────────┐
│        test        │           ts            │ microseconds │
│      varchar       │        timestamp        │    int64     │
├────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┼──────────────┤
│ no_trailing_zeroes │ 2024-11-28 23:59:00.312 │       312000 │
│ trailing_zeroes    │ 2024-11-28 23:59:00.312 │       312000 │
└────────────────────┴─────────────────────────┴──────────────┘
```
Actual result:
```
┌────────────────────┬────────────────────────────┬──────────────┐
│        test        │             ts             │ microseconds │
│      varchar       │         timestamp          │    int64     │
├────────────────────┼────────────────────────────┼──────────────┤
│ no_trailing_zeroes │ 2024-11-28 23:59:00.000312 │          312 │
│ trailing_zeroes    │ 2024-11-28 23:59:00.312    │       312000 │
└────────────────────┴────────────────────────────┴──────────────┘
```

### OS:

Mac OS

### DuckDB Version:

v1.2.2 7c039464e4

### DuckDB Client:

cli

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_timestamps (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, description VARCHAR, timestamp_col TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_timestamps (description, timestamp_col) VALUES ('no_trailing_zeroes', '2024-11-28 23:59:00.312'), ('trailing_zeroes', '2024-11-28 23:59:00.312000'), ('null_value', NULL);
CREATE TABLE additional_data (ref_id INTEGER, value FLOAT, FOREIGN KEY (ref_id) REFERENCES test_timestamps(id));
INSERT INTO additional_data (ref_id, value) VALUES (1, 123.456), (2, 789.012), (3, NULL);
SELECT t.description, t.timestamp_col, EXTRACT(MICROSECOND FROM t.timestamp_col) AS microseconds FROM test_timestamps t LEFT JOIN additional_data a ON t.id = a.ref_id;
